Question: Use Green’s theorem to calculate line integral $$\int_{C} \text{sin} ( x^{2} ) d x + ( 3 x - y ) d y ,$$ where C is a right triangle with vertices $$( -1 , 2 ) ,$$ $$( 4 , 2 ) ,$$ and $$( 4 , 5 )$$ oriented counterclockwise.
Find the area of the region enclosed by the curve with parameterization $$\mathbf{\text{r}} ( t ) = \langle \text{sin} t \text{cos} t , \text{sin} t \rangle , 0 \le t \le \pi .$$
Calculate the flux of $$\mathbf{\text{F}} ( x , y ) = \langle x^{3} , y^{3} \rangle$$ across a unit circle oriented counterclockwise.
Find a stream function for vector field $$\mathbf{\text{F}} ( x , y ) = \langle x \text{sin} y , \text{cos} y \rangle .$$
Is the function $$f ( x , y ) = e^{x + 5 y}$$ harmonic?

Calculate integral $$\int_{\partial D} \mathbf{\text{F}} \cdotp d \mathbf{\text{r}} ,$$ where D is the annulus given by the polar inequalities $$2 \le r \le 5 , 0 \le \theta \le 2 \pi ,$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{F}} ( x , y ) = \langle x^{3} , 5 x + e^{y} \text{sin} y \rangle .$$
